seo_title: Current Time in São Paulo — BRT (UTC-3)
seo_description: Current local time in São Paulo, Brazil. Brasília Time (BRT, UTC-3) year-round — no DST since 2019. Live clock, time difference to New York, London, Tokyo, and business hours.

faq.question: What time is it in São Paulo right now?
faq.answer: São Paulo, Brazil uses Brasília Time (BRT, UTC-3) year-round. Brazil permanently abolished daylight saving time in April 2019, so UTC-3 is fixed all year. The current local time is shown live at the top of this page. São Paulo is the largest city in the Southern Hemisphere and South America's financial capital.

faq.question: What time zone is São Paulo in?
faq.answer: São Paulo is in the Brasília Time (BRT) zone, which is UTC-3. This timezone covers southeastern Brazil including São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and the capital Brasília. Since Brazil abolished DST in 2019, BRT (UTC-3) is the fixed year-round offset for São Paulo. The IANA identifier is America/Sao_Paulo.

faq.question: Does São Paulo observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Brazil abolished daylight saving time by Decree No. 9,772 on April 25, 2019. São Paulo now remains on BRT (UTC-3) year-round. Before 2019, São Paulo would shift to BRST (UTC-2) during Southern Hemisphere summer (October–February). The elimination of DST makes São Paulo's time predictable and consistent for international scheduling.

faq.question: What is the time difference between São Paulo and New York?
faq.answer: São Paulo (BRT, UTC-3) is 2 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (November–March). During US Eastern Daylight Time (March–November), the gap narrows to 1 hour. Since Brazil no longer observes DST, the difference shifts only when the US changes its clocks. When it is 9:00 AM in New York (EST), it is 11:00 AM in São Paulo.

faq.question: What is the time difference between São Paulo and London?
faq.answer: São Paulo (BRT, UTC-3) is 3 hours behind London during GMT (October–March) and 4 hours behind during BST (March–October). When it is 12:00 PM in London (GMT), it is 9:00 AM in São Paulo. Since Brazil no longer uses DST, the gap changes only when the UK adjusts its clocks.

faq.question: What is the best time to call São Paulo from the US?
faq.answer: From New York (EST), the best window is 9:00 AM–3:00 PM EST, which corresponds to 11:00 AM–5:00 PM BRT in São Paulo — catching the full São Paulo business day. During EDT (summer), 9:00 AM EDT = 10:00 AM BRT. From Los Angeles (PST), call 6:00 AM–12:00 PM PST to reach São Paulo 9:00 AM–3:00 PM BRT.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in São Paulo?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in São Paulo are 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM BRT, Monday through Friday. Banks operate 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. São Paulo's financial district (Faria Lima and Itaim Bibi) often runs 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM in banking and finance. São Paulo is Latin America's largest financial center — home to B3 (Brazil's stock exchange), which opens at 10:00 AM BRT and closes at 6:00 PM BRT.

faq.question: What is the time difference between São Paulo and Tokyo?
faq.answer: São Paulo (BRT, UTC-3) is 12 hours behind Tokyo (JST, UTC+9). When it is 9:00 AM in São Paulo, it is 9:00 PM in Tokyo. Japan does not observe DST and Brazil no longer does either, so this 12-hour difference is constant year-round. Scheduling calls between São Paulo and Tokyo typically requires one side to work outside standard business hours.

content_blocks.title: São Paulo Time Zone — BRT (UTC-3)
content_blocks.content: São Paulo uses Brasília Time (BRT, UTC-3), 3 hours behind Coordinated Universal Time. Brazil permanently abolished daylight saving time in 2019 (Decree No. 9,772), so this offset is fixed year-round. São Paulo is the largest city in the Southern Hemisphere and South America's financial hub, home to over 22 million people in its metropolitan area. São Paulo shares BRT with Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and most of southeastern Brazil. The IANA identifier is America/Sao_Paulo.

content_blocks.title: São Paulo vs World Cities — Time Differences
content_blocks.content: São Paulo (BRT, UTC-3) compared to major cities: New York — 2h ahead (EST) / 1h ahead (EDT); London — 3h behind (GMT) / 4h behind (BST); Paris/Berlin — 4h behind (CET) / 5h behind (CEST); Dubai — 7h behind (GST); Singapore — 11h behind (SGT); Tokyo — 12h behind (JST); Sydney — 13–14h behind (AEDT/AEST). All São Paulo offsets are fixed since Brazil no longer observes DST — only the comparison city's DST changes the gap.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and Financial Markets in São Paulo
content_blocks.content: São Paulo is Latin America's largest financial center. B3 (Bolsa Brasil Balcão, formerly BM&FBovespa) — Brazil's stock exchange — operates 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM BRT on weekdays, with pre-opening at 9:45 AM. The Faria Lima corridor (São Paulo's Wall Street) runs 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM for banking and asset management. Standard office hours are 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Banks: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. For international coordination: New York overlap is 11:00 AM–3:00 PM BRT (9:00 AM–1:00 PM EST); London overlap is 12:00–4:00 PM BRT (2:00–6:00 PM GMT in winter).

timezone_facts.dst_notes: Brazil abolished DST in April 2019 (Decree No. 9,772). BRT (UTC-3) is fixed year-round.

eeat_footer: Last updated: March 2026 | Verified by whattime.city Editorial Team | Timezone data sourced from IANA Time Zone Database (America/Sao_Paulo). Brazil DST abolished by Decree No. 9,772, April 25, 2019.
